subject: SDN-C db schema/sdnctl not reliably being created
commit_message:
SDN-C db schema/sdnctl not reliably being created

Change-Id: I7fa43e090c11956eb68f89ec65bfe0ee7922c78e
Signed-off-by: Mohammadreza Pasandideh <mohammadreza.pasandideh@amdocs.com>
Issue-ID: OOM-887

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified-1

Doesn't seem to come up in my environment.

/opt/onap/sdnc/bin/installSdncDb.sh: line 25: ${-dev-sdnc-db-0.sdnc-dbhost.onap}: bad substitution

I have it combined with these patches:
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/45719/
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/44737/

Patch Set 1:

It actually seems to be running but that error on line 25 is concerning.

dev-sdnc-0                                                        2/2       Running             0          21m
dev-sdnc-ansible-server-7c6ccbbc79-k87fc                          1/1       Running             0          21m
dev-sdnc-db-0                                                     2/2       Running             0          21m
dev-sdnc-dgbuilder-c6b7f7f8b-lxs8q                                1/1       Running             0          21m
dev-sdnc-dmaap-listener-5f76485746-lt2w9                          1/1       Running             0          21m
dev-sdnc-portal-956dd5df5-4p6pp                                   1/1       Running             0          21m
dev-sdnc-ueb-listener-54fff7d465-5pkl4                            1/1       Running             0          21m

Not sure how to proceed with this one.

Patch Set 1:

discussed with James.

Rather than changing the MYSQL_HOST in the installSdncDb.sh, we will change the variable in sdnc pod, so all communications will be directed to db-0. 

Currently, MYSQL_HOST env variable, inside sdnc pod is set to sdnc_dbhost service which is doing round robin and 50% of write operations should fail, as they hit db-1 (read only db).

To avoid this, we plan to change the MYSQL_HOST in the pod to point to db-0.
Going to send a new patch set soon.
Change has been successfully merged by Mandeep Khinda
